scores.correctness_notes: Covers the basic change/task model and waiting patterns, but misses key details from the gold standard: no mention of the REST API endpoints (GET /v1/changes/{id}, /wait, filtering), no mention of pebble.state persistence, no mention of notices/change-update, incomplete change structure details (spawn-time, ready-time, progress counters). The Python API coverage is partial.
scores.hallucination_notes: container.get_pebble() is not a standard ops Container method. client.start_services() returning a ChangeID directly is questionable — the gold standard shows it waits automatically. The ExecProcess.wait_output() pattern is roughly correct but container.exec() details may be slightly off from actual API.
